mechanism_narrative: Higher real retail sales indicate robust consumer spending, driving corporate earnings and supporting equity prices over a one-month horizon. The signal is always active and directionally positive.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS level and next 20-day SPX return is less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5%, it signals labor market weakness and rising recession risk, which depresses equity valuations over the following quarter. The signal is triggered by a threshold crossing.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, the average 60-day forward SPX return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) over the historical period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and payrolls signal a broad economic contraction, which drives negative equity returns over the next quarter. The joint condition amplifies the bearish signal.
failure_condition: In episodes where both INDPRO < 100 and PAYEMS < 159,000, the 60-day forward SPX return is negative less than 60% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening regime defined by the federal funds rate above 4%, further rate increases compress equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative forward returns. The signal is active only in this high-rate regime.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4%, the correlation between changes in FEDFUNDS and next 20-day SPX returns is zero or positive.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production reflects expanding economic activity, which supports corporate earnings and lifts equity prices. This always-on signal has a positive directional effect over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO level with next 20-day returns is below 0.05 over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rising CPI signals persistent inflation, which erodes real earnings and prompts tighter monetary policy, both negative for equity valuations over the short to medium term. This always-on signal has a negative directional effect.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year correlation between CPIAUCSL level and next 20-day SPX return is positive or insignificant at 10% level.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in retail sales signals sudden consumer pullback, which quickly depresses equity prices within a week. The signal is triggered only when the decline exceeds 1%.
failure_condition: In months following a >1% MoM decline in RSAFS, the 5-day forward SPX return is positive more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is low and industrial production is strong, the combination of accommodative monetary policy and expanding supply-side activity creates a favorable environment for equities over the next quarter. The joint condition is necessary for the positive signal.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with supply-side expansion
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS < 3% and INDPRO > 105, the 60-day forward SPX return is not positive at a statistically significant level (one-tailed t-test p > 0.05).
